name: File Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 7-quart stock pot, bring the whole chicken to a boil until the meat separates from the bone.
2. Remove the cooked meat and return the bones and all organs (excluding the liver) to the pot. Simmer for half an hour to make a stock. Strain the stock and skim off any excess fat. Return the strained stock to the pot.
3. In a cast iron skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add flour and stir with a wooden spoon until the mixture forms a dark brown roux.
4. Add chopped onions, celery, scallions, and bell pepper to the skillet. Sauté for five minutes.
5. Pour in the prepared stock and return it to the pot.
6. In the same skillet, lightly brown the Andouille sausage with garlic, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper.
7. Drain any excess fat from the sausage and add the meat along with one bay leaf to the stock.
8. Simmer the mixture for one hour at a very low boil.
9. Just before serving, sprinkle file powder over the gumbo and serve it hot over a bowl of rice.
Note: This recipe is prepared by John Whelan from Roxanne Enterprises in New Orleans, Louisiana.
